# AGENTS.md — `manuscript/`

**Publication:** DOI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20419536 · configured in [`config.yaml`](config.yaml) · claim matrix [`../docs/_audit/pymdp_lean_manuscript_matrix_2026-05-21.csv`](../docs/_audit/pymdp_lean_manuscript_matrix_2026-05-21.csv)

## Current state

* **29 front/body/part section files** (front matter `0A`; Part I 1A–1C; Part II 2A–2J; Part III 3A–3B;
  Part IV 4A–4E; Part V 5A–5D; Part VI 6A–6C).
* **8 supplements** (`S01`–`S08`).
* **1 auto-generated bibliography** (`99_bibliography.md`) sourced from
  [`refs/citations.yaml`](refs/citations.yaml).  Live citation and
  rendered-bibliography counts are generated by
  [`../scripts/manuscript_variables.py`](../scripts/manuscript_variables.py)
  into `output/data/manuscript_variables.json`; do not hand-maintain
  those counts in onboarding prose.
* **Every numbered theorem row** carries a live Lean companion
  (5 `proved`, 1 `forwarder`, 3 `boundary`, 11 `witness`; **zero
  `deferred`, zero `sketch`, zero strict `sorry`**).  The 5 `proved`
  rows are NOT all proofs-as-named: each carries a `faithfulness:`
  field in `refs/labels.yaml` — only **2 `substantive`** (cor_4_2,
  cor_4_3); the other **3 are `statement-restricted`** (prop_6_1
  proves membership not e-flatness; prop_6_2 a conditional KL equality
  not minimality; prop_7_1 an `Iff.rfl` unfolding not the Schmidt-rank
  equivalence).  Never quote the bare "5 proved" without that split.
  Source of
  truth: [`refs/labels.yaml`](refs/labels.yaml); per-row audit:
  [`../docs/reference/veridical_status.md`](../docs/reference/veridical_status.md).
* The `MathlibRefinementRoadmap.md` under
  `lean/ActinfPolicyEntanglement/` tracks the per-witness
  Mathlib4 discharge plan; references in §3B, §S05, and §6C point at
  that file.
* §4B contains live `K > 2`, configured long-horizon, and
  m-projection-revertibility subsections that support the analytical
  theorems in [[SECREF:heterogeneous]] and [[SECREF:geometry]].

### Citation history (do **not** re-add entries — bibliography is curated)

The bibliography was reconciled in round 3:

* `waade-2025` ("As One and Many") — replaces an earlier typo-keyed
  entry; cited from [[SECREF:connections_multi_agent_geometry]].
* `friston-2024-federated` (federated inference / belief broadcasting)
  — added as a distinct entry alongside `friston-2024` (RGM);
  cited from [[SECREF:connections_multi_agent_geometry]].
* See [`refs/README.md`](refs/README.md) for the **slug-vs-year**
  convention (preprint year in the slug; publication year in the
  `year:` field — e.g. `friston-2024` carries `year: 2025`).

### When the registry says one thing and the prose says another

Always trust [`refs/labels.yaml`](refs/labels.yaml) and
[`refs/citations.yaml`](refs/citations.yaml).  The renderer extracts
status, Lean source, and citation metadata from the registry at every
render — drift between prose and registry is a manuscript bug, not a
registry bug.

## Authoring rules

1. **One topic per file.**  Don't merge sections; the file boundary is
   the section boundary.  Break a section in two only if the manuscript
   itself does.
2. **Lexical order = render order.**  Files are concatenated in
   `os.listdir`-sorted order.  Numeric prefixes (`00_`, `01_`, …,
   `S01_`, …, `99_`) keep this stable.
3. **Cross-reference Lean.**  When stating a numbered theorem, link
   the file under
   [`../lean/ActinfPolicyEntanglement/`](../lean/ActinfPolicyEntanglement/)
   and name the Lean theorem (e.g. `entanglement_decomposition`).
4. **Cross-reference figures.**  Use relative paths to
   `../output/figures/<name>.png` so the rendered PDF picks them up
   from the same directory the pipeline collects.
5. **No hardcoded numbers (CI-gated).**  Every numeric value (grid
   count, sweep range, agreement tolerance, sample size, sentinel-λ
   value, rollout horizon, seed) reaches the prose via `[[VAR:key]]`
   against `output/data/manuscript_variables.json`.  The single
   source of truth for those numbers is
   [`../src/simulation/hyperparameters.py`](../src/simulation/hyperparameters.py).
   The validator
   [`scripts/validate_manuscript.py`](../scripts/validate_manuscript.py)
   calls
   `manuscript.validation.find_hardcoded_numeric_literals` and fails
   CI on patterns like `121-point grid`, `21 grid points`,
   `seed = 0`, `T = 10 steps` typed as raw digits.  Defined symbols
   (`K`, `λ`) and small mathematical constants ($\log 2$, $1/2$,
   $\pi$) are exempt.  See
   [`../docs/guides/styleguide/manuscript-variables.md`](../docs/guides/styleguide/manuscript-variables.md)
   for the regex catalog + replacement recipes.
   The same validator also scans paper-facing source fields — headings,
   `refs/labels.yaml` section titles, figure captions / short labels,
   theorem names, and equation names / LaTeX — for raw display labels.
   Use `[[SECREF:...]]`, `[[THMREF:...]]`, `[[FIGREF:...]]`, and
   `[[EQREF:...]]`, or neutral headings, instead of hand-written
   `§9.5`, `Theorem 5.1`, `Figure 7`, or `Eq. (3)`.
6. **Math conventions.**
   * Natural log unless explicitly stated.
   * Joint distributions use `q(π¹, …, π^K)`; marginals use `q^k`.
   * Coupling parameter is `λ` (italic, lowercase).
   * KL divergence is `D_KL(p ‖ q)` (LaTeX `\KL{p}{q}` if you load
     [`preamble.md`](preamble.md)).
7. **References.**  Add a key to
   [`refs/citations.yaml`](refs/citations.yaml) (topic, authors, year,
   venue, optional DOI URL).  Cite from the body with Pandoc-style tokens
   `[@lastname-yyyy]` (see [`refs/README.md`](refs/README.md)).  Do **not**
   hand-maintain numbered lists in
   [`99_bibliography.md`](99_bibliography.md) — it is auto-filled from
   `[[CITELIST:all]]` after injection.

## Adding a section

1. Pick a free numeric prefix (e.g. inserting `04a_` between `04_` and
   `05_`).  If the new content is a peer of the existing sections,
   bump the existing numbers — careful, this changes filenames.
2. Add the new file to the table in [`README.md`](README.md).
3. Run `./run.sh --project actinf_policy_entanglement_lean --pipeline`
   from the template root and inspect the PDF.

## Translating prose ⇄ Lean / Python

Whenever you state or refine a theorem in prose, the parallel changes
should be:

* Lean: edit
  [`../lean/ActinfPolicyEntanglement/<Module>.lean`](../lean/ActinfPolicyEntanglement/),
  ensure `lake build` stays green.
* Python: edit [`../src/<module>.py`](../src/), add or update tests in
  [`../tests/test_<module>.py`](../tests/), keep coverage ≥ 95%.
* Docs: update the relevant page under [`../docs/`](../docs/).

## Style sheet (terse)

* Use American English in prose, captions, and section titles.  Do not
  rename historical code identifiers or exact citation titles solely
  for spelling.
* Equations with their own line use `$$ ... $$` (display) — never
  add `\tag{...}` or `\label{...}` by hand; the renderer
  ([`../src/manuscript/equation_numbering.py`](../src/manuscript/equation_numbering.py))
  auto-numbers every display equation as `S.K` (S = section, K =
  within-section position).  Use `[[EQ:label]]` to insert a
  registry-defined equation; cross-reference with `[[EQREF:label]]`.
* Use `**bold**` for theorem statements and `*italic*` for new term
  introductions.
* Keep paragraphs ≤ 6 sentences.  Long tables go in appendices.
